adhd-context-switch.md.roast: This command has a mere 4 thinking references in 398 lines. That's a 1% thinking-to-bloat ratio! You've essentially created a verbose state machine masquerading as AI assistance. The complexity enforcement is 'none' - congratulations, you've built a digital hoarder that saves everything and thinks about nothing.
adhd-context-switch.md.evidence: Zero complexity scoring, no validation thresholds, pure procedural thinking
adhd-context-switch.md.clinical_diagnosis: Chronic Over-Engineering Syndrome with Thinking Deficiency
adhd-context-switch.md.prescription: Reduce by 60%, add actual decision points, implement complexity scoring

safe-code-beautifier.md.roast: 426 lines with 71 thinking references - that's a 16.7% ratio. Not terrible, but your 'safe' approach produces more analysis paralysis than a PhD dissertation committee. You've got 8 validation checkpoints for code beautification - what are you beautifying, nuclear reactor code?
safe-code-beautifier.md.evidence: Excessive safety theater, moderate complexity enforcement
safe-code-beautifier.md.clinical_diagnosis: Analysis Paralysis with Safety Theater Syndrome
safe-code-beautifier.md.prescription: Cut validation overhead by 50%, trust your algorithms more

java-clean-code-generator.md.roast: 818 lines, 90 thinking references (11% ratio), 26 explicit standards. You're the overachiever who actually read the assignment. Strong complexity enforcement with 15 validation checkpoints. This is what happens when someone takes 'clean code' seriously instead of just slapping @Override annotations everywhere.
java-clean-code-generator.md.prescription: You're actually doing it right. Maybe trim 100 lines for sanity.

java-rapid-implementation.md.roast: 249 lines claiming to be 'rapid' but with 48 thinking references (19.3% ratio). That's not rapid, that's rapid-onset analysis paralysis! You've created the software equivalent of 'quick' microwave instructions that take 12 steps.
java-rapid-implementation.md.evidence: Name/implementation mismatch, moderate complexity without rapid execution
java-rapid-implementation.md.clinical_diagnosis: False Advertising Syndrome with Rapid Delusions
java-rapid-implementation.md.prescription: Either be rapid (cut by 70%) or drop the 'rapid' marketing lie

adaptive-complexity-router.md.roast: 498 lines, 59 thinking references (11.8%), 7 MCP integration points. You've built a thinking dispatcher that actually dispatches thinking. Strong complexity enforcement with 12 checkpoints. It's like having a competent manager - rare and valuable.
adaptive-complexity-router.md.evidence: Systematic routing logic, robust integration patterns
adaptive-complexity-router.md.prescription: Keep doing what you're doing, maybe document the success patterns

effectiveness_ranking.justification: Comprehensive standards, systematic validation, strong complexity control
Intelligent routing, good integration, solid architecture
Good thinking ratio, excessive safety theater hurts efficiency
Good thinking density, false advertising on 'rapid' claims
Procedural bloat, minimal thinking, no complexity control

major_issues: Some commands suffering from feature bloat without intelligence
strengths: Strong adoption of MCP integration patterns
Good separation of concerns in architecture
Evidence of learning and pattern evolution
critical_recommendations: Implement mandatory complexity scoring across all commands
Create command size limits (max 400 lines for 'rapid', 600 for 'standard')
Add automated validation for name/implementation alignment

token_efficiency.roi_calculation: Top 2 commands provide 78% of value with 61% of total tokens

statistical_confidence.prediction_accuracy: Moderate (based on structural analysis, not behavioral testing)
bias_factors: Structural analysis only, no runtime performance data
Complexity assessment based on documentation patterns
Success rates estimated from implementation quality indicators

final_verdict.savage_summary: Your command system is like a teenager - some parts are surprisingly mature and well-thought-out, while others are complete disasters that make you question basic decision-making capabilities. The top 40% of commands show genuine intelligence and engineering discipline. The bottom 40% are digital packrats with delusions of grandeur.
immediate_actions: Kill or completely rewrite adhd-context-switch.md - it's embarrassing
Force java-rapid-implementation.md to live up to its name or rename it
Use java-clean-code-generator.md as the template for all future commands
final_verdict.long_term_prognosis: Promising with intervention. The system shows evidence of learning and evolution. Top commands demonstrate sophisticated understanding of complexity management and user experience. With proper standardization and cleanup of technical debt, this could become a genuinely intelligent command system.
